explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: The Chewy Privacy Policy mentions third-party service use for analytics. It also mentions third-party service use for service evaluation.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: The Chewy Privacy Policy provides evidence for **external_accountability**, **governance**, and **privacy**. It supports **governance** by outlining policies for data collection and user notice, and by mentioning auditing for compliance. Evidence for **privacy** is found in the policy's definition of profiling systems and its mention of opt-out mechanisms for user data. The policy also touches on **external_accountability** through its mention of third-party service use for analytics and service evaluation.
relevant_sources.title: Chewy Privacy Policy

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: A PwC report highlights the critical role of a responsible AI policy and governance frameworks in Chewy's AI deployment. The Chewy Privacy Policy outlines policies for data collection and user notice, and mentions auditing for compliance. Additionally, a report references that AI-driven autonomous decision-making and the use of reinforcement learning necessitate governance over AI systems and their learning processes.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This PwC report, "AI Is No Longer an Option—It's the Future of Retail," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The report highlights Chewy's strategic integration of AI, emphasizing the critical role of a responsible AI policy, governance frameworks, and trust in their AI deployment. It also notes the importance of ethical AI deployment and transparency as integral to their business strategy.
relevant_sources.title: AI Is No Longer an Option—It's the Future of Retail

relevant_sources.summary: The Chewy Privacy Policy provides evidence for **external_accountability**, **governance**, and **privacy**. Evidence for **privacy** is found in the policy's definition of profiling systems and its mention of opt-out mechanisms for user data. The policy also touches on **external_accountability** through its mention of third-party service use for analytics and service evaluation.
relevant_sources.title: Chewy Privacy Policy

relevant_sources.summary: The "Chewy CHAMP Autonomous Mobile Picking Challenge" report provides evidence for the **governance** pillar. This is because the challenge involves AI-driven autonomous decision-making and the use of reinforcement learning for performance improvement, both of which necessitate governance over the AI systems and their learning processes.
relevant_sources.title: Chewy CHAMP Autonomous Mobile Picking Challenge

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.findings: The Chewy Privacy Policy defines profiling systems. It also mentions opt-out mechanisms for user data.
privacy.path_to_improvement: Publish AI-specific privacy impact assessments or data handling procedures.

transparency.findings: A PwC report highlights transparency as critical in Chewy's AI deployment. It notes the importance of transparency as integral to Chewy's business strategy.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This PwC report, "AI Is No Longer an Option—It's the Future of Retail," provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The report highlights Chewy's strategic integration of AI, emphasizing the critical role of a responsible AI policy, governance frameworks, and trust in their AI deployment. It also notes the importance of ethical AI deployment and transparency as integral to their business strategy.
relevant_sources.title: AI Is No Longer an Option—It's the Future of Retail

summary.overall_findings: Chewy's Privacy Policy defines profiling systems and outlines opt-out mechanisms for user data. These practices contribute to the documentation of 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars, with transparency, governance, and external accountability also addressed at the policy level. For instance, published materials highlight transparency as critical in AI deployment and note the importance of governance frameworks. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar, based on a review of 8 publicly available sources.
